raw_result.description: Airtable provides a REST API for creating, reading, updating, and deleting records in Airtable bases and for managing related workspace resources.
raw_result.auth_notes: Airtable’s authentication documentation describes personal access tokens and OAuth access tokens; it also notes legacy API keys are deprecated, so they are not included as a current documented method.
raw_result.gating_reason: Airtable’s developer billing plans page documents self-serve Free, Team, Business, and Enterprise Scale plans, indicating API access is available on a self-serve free tier, with some advanced endpoints/features restricted to higher plans.

api_surface.breadth: Public Web API docs cover core record CRUD plus metadata and enterprise/workspace-related resources, with separate pages for endpoints, webhooks, metadata, comments, attachments, and enterprise features.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Airtable first-party MCP server or Model Context Protocol page was verified from the gathered evidence, so this is treated as not evidenced; human review is required for this field.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official rate-limit docs indicate 5 requests per second per base, plus 50 requests per second for personal access tokens/service accounts, with 429 responses requiring a 30-second wait before retrying.
raw_result.notable_signals: Well-structured first-party developer docs, explicit auth guidance, explicit billing-plan mapping, and documented rate limits all support fast toolkit implementation; legacy API keys are explicitly deprecated.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The structured field api_surface.mcp_exists could not be supported by first-party evidence already gathered: no official Airtable MCP page was verified, but absence from the inspected pages alone is not conclusive.

result.auth_notes: Airtable’s authentication documentation describes personal access tokens and OAuth access tokens; it also notes legacy API keys are deprecated, so they are not included as a current documented method.
